title: Tuberaria plantaginea
intro: Tuberaria plantaginea, also known as the Rock Rose or Maritime Rock Rose, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tuberaria plantaginea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tuberaria plantaginea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and fever.
taxonomy: Tuberaria plantaginea is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region, including the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. The plant is often classified as a succulent due to its thick, fleshy leaves that store water. Tuberaria plantaginea is a member of the genus Tuberaria, which comprises several species of plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Tuberaria plantaginea as a succulent and a member of the Cistaceae family is widely accepted by botanists and plant taxonomists.

In some traditional medicine practices, Tuberaria plantaginea is used to alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing.
